parsed_review.summary: CP00-884 is a descriptor-only RP29 Commercial Readiness UI fixture permission/audit binding slice (RP29.P05.M04.S16-RP29.P05.M06.S13, 40 units, risk B). Read-only inspection of packages/commercial/src/ui.js, registry.js, validators.js, the test suite, and both contract files confirms the slice is descriptor-only: every section row and attestation sets runtime_execution=false, permission_decision_written=false, audit_event_written=false, runtime_receipt_emitted=false, real_client_data_included=false, and credential_or_secret_included=false, with only synthetic tenant/matter identifiers (e.g. tenant-synthetic-alpha, matter-synthetic-release-readiness). Deliverable distribution (test 5 / hermes_evidence 2 / implementation 14 / fixture 13 / claude_review 2 / security_audit 4 = 40) and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice 5 / Permission And Audit Binding 22 / Synthetic Fixture Set 13) independently recompute to the baseline. The CP00-883 P3 stale shared-UI handoff text is remediated to phase-agnostic copy (ui.js:137, contract line 3119) and is enforced by both a validator assertion (validators.js:1637) and a test (model.test.js:517). Validators carry genuine assertions for descriptor-only boundaries, audit-hint no-write, security-trimming omission, Hermes receipt-free evidence, and Claude read-only role; tests load the real on-disk contract. Authority boundary holds: Claude is a read-only reviewer and not final approver, human approval is required for runtime opening, Hermes is control-plane evidence only (emits_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), and the pack does not claim runtime/enterprise trust (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false). The pending-review placeholders in claude-review-result.json, construction-inspection.json, and adjudication.md are the expected pre-receipt baseline that downstream normalization will flip. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 records that this verdict derives from static read-only inspection (no Bash): targeted command exit codes are as reported in command-evidence.json and the full deterministic validation ladder must still pass during normalization before production_ready is promoted. Pack closeout is supported; goal closeout is not blocked by this receipt.

findings.title: PASS derives from static read-only inspection; full validation ladder deferred to downstream normalization
findings.details: As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash), I verified implementation, tests, validators, contracts, and the no-write/no-real-data boundaries by direct file inspection, but did not re-execute any validation command. The targeted exit_code:0 results recorded in command-evidence.json (commercial syntax check, commercial model/service tests, npm run rp29:commercial:validate) are as reported, not independently re-run in this review. command-evidence.json:19 itself states the full deterministic validation ladder remains required after review normalization. The manifest correctly keeps production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false, so nothing is over-claimed; this is a provenance note, not a code defect.
findings.recommendation: Keep production_ready=false until downstream normalization executes and records the full validation ladder green (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run spec:requirements:validate, npm run weighted:validate, npm run fullplan:validate, npm run goal:closeout:validate, npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) and normalizes this receipt into claude-review-result.json / adjudication.md / construction-inspection.json. No source change is required for this finding.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational, non-blocking; tracked for downstream normalization. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
